title: Overview Focus Mode Layout Rescue and Enterprise Information Architecture Cleanup
root_cause_of_messy_layout: The full six-card focus-mode chooser was rendered inside the narrow right intelligence rail.
Long focus descriptions, snapshot actions, selected parcel context, and stats competed at equal visual weight in a sidebar-width area.
The map column felt squeezed because the primary focus-selection workflow was visually buried in a narrow secondary panel.

focus_chooser_relocation.summary: Moved the review-focus chooser into a compact command strip above the SceneView in the main map column.
ui_behavior: Compact horizontal/grid buttons with icon, short title, one-line purpose, and active state.
No long descriptions in the right sidebar.
No auto-scroll or horizontal scrollbar behavior added.
Stable data-testid attributes added for focus-mode smoke verification.

right_panel_redesign.summary: Reworked Overview intelligence rail into a current-focus summary instead of a full focus chooser.
clutter_reduction: Right panel now shows only the selected focus mode and relevant actions.
Recommended layers are shown as compact chips.
Stats are limited to concise, focus-specific signals.
Advanced context is collapsed under More Details.
Redundant Next Action block and duplicate snapshot action in the selected parcel card were removed.

layer_organization_changes.summary: Focus modes recommend and safely enable relevant existing layers without removing advanced layer access.

snapshot_report_map_screenshot_verificat.save_snapshot_behavior: Browser smoke clicked Save Snapshot for Report from the visible DOM. SceneView screenshot capture timed out in the browser session and the snapshot was still saved safely.
notes: The report correctly keeps the snapshot/report workflow usable when ArcGIS screenshot capture is unavailable.
No fake map image was generated.

model_wording_safety_changes.summary: Overview focus language separates observed development activity from aggregate-only internal model governance.
safe_wording_used: Internal model research is not a live prediction layer.
Model outputs remain hidden and internal-only.
Focus mode recommends layers and report context. It does not create model predictions, parcel scores, or official capacity findings.
model_wording_safety_changes.safety_grep_note: Existing aggregate research-status hook still contains internal class labels for non-parcel aggregate governance; they were not surfaced as parcel-level Overview or Planning Snapshot outputs.

browser_smoke_results.focus_switch_result: Constraints & Risk became active and the right panel showed FEMA Flood Zones, Flood Constraints, School Utilization Seed, and snapshot actions.
browser_smoke_results.browser_console_errors: No new app errors observed during targeted DOM/text smoke. Known ArcGIS/browser automation timing around screenshot capture produced a handled map snapshot unavailable placeholder.

remaining_known_issues: ArcGIS SceneView screenshot capture may time out in local/browser automation; the report handles this with a clean unavailable placeholder.
Focus modes recommend and enable relevant layers but do not hide every advanced layer control; advanced layer access remains preserved by design.
The right panel can sit below the first browser viewport at some desktop sizes because the map remains primary; the layout no longer places the full focus chooser in that narrow rail.
recommended_next_step: Continue with targeted visual QA on a full desktop viewport and, if desired, add a lightweight automated UI test for the Overview focus strip and Planning Snapshot placeholder path.
